skill.name: firebase-hosting-basics
skill.description: Skill for working with Firebase Hosting (Classic). Use this when you want to deploy static web apps, Single Page Apps (SPAs), or simple microservices. Do NOT use for Firebase App Hosting.
skill.summary: Deploy and manage static websites and SPAs on Firebase Hosting with global CDN and automatic SSL.
skill.author: firebase

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The flagged 'external_commands' are markdown documentation examples showing firebase-tools CLI commands for users to run manually. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections matched on configuration JSON examples, not actual crypto code. The 'network' flag was triggered by a localhost URL in documentation. No executable code or malicious patterns exist in these documentation files.

risk_factor_evidence.reasoning: All flagged locations are markdown code blocks documenting CLI commands for users to execute manually. No programmatic command execution occurs within the skill itself.

content.user_title: Deploy and host web apps on Firebase
content.value_statement: Firebase Hosting provides fast, secure deployment for static sites and SPAs with global CDN and automatic SSL. This skill guides you through configuration, deployment commands, and preview channel management.
actual_capabilities: Configure firebase.json for hosting behavior, redirects, and rewrites
Deploy static sites and SPAs to Firebase Hosting with CLI commands
Create and manage preview channels for testing changes
Set up custom headers and URL routing rules
Clone preview channel versions to production
Emulate hosting locally for testing before deployment
limitations: Only supports Firebase Hosting (Classic), not Firebase App Hosting for SSR frameworks
Requires Firebase CLI and project setup before use
Does not handle backend function deployment beyond hosting rewrites configuration

use_cases.title: Deploy a React SPA
use_cases.description: Configure and deploy a React single-page application with proper rewrite rules for client-side routing.
use_cases.target_user: Frontend developers building SPAs with React, Vue, or Angular

use_cases.title: Set Up Preview Channels
use_cases.description: Create temporary preview URLs for testing feature branches before merging to production.
use_cases.target_user: Teams implementing CI/CD workflows with GitHub Actions

use_cases.title: Configure Custom Headers
use_cases.description: Add security headers, CORS policies, and cache control to your Firebase Hosting deployment.
use_cases.target_user: DevOps engineers optimizing web performance and security

prompt_templates.title: Basic Deployment Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up Firebase Hosting for my static website. I need to configure the public directory, set up clean URLs, and deploy to production.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner deploying their first static site

prompt_templates.title: SPA Routing Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Configure firebase.json to handle client-side routing for my Vue.js SPA. All routes should serve index.html except API calls.
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer setting up single-page application routing

prompt_templates.title: Preview Channel Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a preview channel called 'feature-checkout' for testing, set it to expire in 3 days, and show me how to clone it to production after approval.
prompt_templates.scenario: Team member testing a feature branch before merge

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Headers and Rewrites
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up custom headers for font files with CORS, configure rewrites for an API proxy to Cloud Functions, and add redirect rules for legacy URLs.

output_examples.input: Configure Firebase Hosting for a React SPA with clean URLs
output: - Add rewrite rule to serve index.html for all routes

output_examples.input: Set up a preview channel for feature testing

best_practices: Always test deployments on preview channels before pushing to production
Use ignore patterns to exclude unnecessary files like node_modules and source maps
Set appropriate cache headers for static assets to improve performance
anti_patterns: Do not deploy sensitive files like .env or credentials to Firebase Hosting
Avoid using the root path for all rewrites without excluding API endpoints
Do not skip preview testing for production deployments

faq.question: What is the difference between Firebase Hosting and Firebase App Hosting?
faq.answer: Firebase Hosting (Classic) serves static files and SPAs with manual CLI deployment. Firebase App Hosting supports full-stack frameworks like Next.js with SSR and automated git-based deployments.

faq.question: How do I enable client-side routing for my SPA?
faq.answer: Add a rewrite rule in firebase.json that sends all requests to index.html. Set source to '**' and destination to '/index.html'.

faq.question: Can I host dynamic content on Firebase Hosting?
faq.answer: Yes, by using rewrites to route requests to Cloud Functions or Cloud Run for dynamic content generation while serving static assets from the CDN.

faq.question: How long do preview channels last?
faq.answer: Preview channels expire after 7 days by default. You can customize the expiration using the --expires flag when deploying.

faq.question: Does Firebase Hosting include SSL certificates?
faq.answer: Yes, Firebase Hosting provides free automatic SSL certificates for all hosted sites with zero configuration required.

faq.question: How do I roll back a bad deployment?
faq.answer: Use 'firebase hosting:releases:list' to see previous versions, then 'firebase hosting:releases:rollback' to restore a previous deployment.
